title: rhus toxicodendron var. eximia
intro: Rhus toxicodendron var. eximia, also known as poison sumac, is a plant species native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its glossy leaves and small, greenish-white flowers. The plant contains urushiol, a potent allergen responsible for causing skin irritation and contact dermatitis in many people. Traditionally, poison sumac has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and psoriasis, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of poison sumac include creams, ointments, and tinctures, which can be used topically to treat skin irritations, although it is essential to note that proper handling and safety precautions are necessary when working with this plant.
